analysis.consistency: High. The descriptive text (color indicating alignment) is clear. The mechanical benefits are standard for a high-level magic item.
analysis.quality: Good. The description is evocative, and the mechanics are clearly defined. The alignment restriction is a specific flavourful constraint.
analysis.mechanics_integrity: High. All listed benefits are quantifiable and usable within standard D&D 5e rules (AC calculation, advantage on saves, bonus to DC/Attack). The attunement restriction based on alignment is a key mechanical gate.
suggested_improvements: The RAW data specifies attunement by 'a sorcerer, warlock, or wizard' in the source line, but the description doesn't explicitly mention class restrictions, only alignment restrictions. For maximal consistency, the description should explicitly state the class requirement if it is a hard mechanical requirement.
Clarify if the alignment restriction (good/neutral/evil) must match the wearer's alignment exactly, or if it simply dictates which of the three versions they can use.

data_points_extracted.name: Robe of the Archmagi
data_points_extracted.save_bonus: Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects
data_points_extracted.special_restriction: Requires attunement corresponding to alignment (white=good, gray=neutral, black=evil); RAW suggests class restriction (Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard).
